requirements: Identify the existing authentication/session boundary and reuse it.
Add authorization check on every protected entrypoint (controller/handler/route).
Preserve current login/logout/session behavior unless explicitly changing it.
Hash passwords with a vetted KDF (argon2id, bcrypt, scrypt). Never reverse-encryptable.
Set session/JWT cookies with HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax or Strict.
Rate-limit credential and reset endpoints; record failed attempts.
Enforce CSRF protection on state-changing authenticated requests.
Avoid timing-side-channel comparisons of secrets and tokens.
Add regression tests for allowed and denied access on every new gate.
security: validate redirect/return-to URLs against an allowlist
prefer short-lived access tokens with refresh rotation
never log password, token, or full session id values
invalidate sessions on password change and on logout
lock account or delay after repeated failures
performance: avoid running KDF on hot paths without caching the verified session
cache permission/role lookups per request, not per database call
verify: lint and unit test changed auth files
manual smoke: login, logout, expired session, unauthorized access
